data.name: Cisco Meraki's Public Workspace
data.summary: Official Cisco Meraki API Postman Collections to manage cloud networks, test webhooks and other helpful utilities
data.description:
[](https://testing-developer.cisco.com/meraki/whats-new/)

**Meraki API - Postman Collections**  
_The power tools for your cloud-managed network_

This workspace provides a comprehensive set of Collections for the Meraki APIs, offering a programmatic way to manage your network's cloud infrastructure. The Dashboard API v1 Collection allows for extensive network management capabilities and receives regular updates to ensure you have access to the latest features and improvements.

Learn more at [Meraki.io](https://developer.cisco.com/meraki/api/)

### Quick Start Guide

#### Setting Up Authentication

Before you can start making API calls, you must set up authentication to securely access your Meraki network data. The Meraki API uses API keys for authentication, ensuring that only authorized users can perform operations.

1. **Obtain Your API Key:**
    - Log in to your [Meraki Dashboard](https://dashboard.meraki.com).
    - Navigate to "Organization" > "API & Webhooks".
    - Copy your API key; if you don't have one, generate a new API key.
2. **Configure Authentication in Postman:**
    - Create an environment for this collection named **`apiKey`**
        1. _The Collection uses Bearer Authentication and will use this environment variable by default._

#### Basic API Calls to Get Started

With authentication set up, you can begin exploring the API's capabilities.  
  
If you haven't already, fork the [Dashboard API v1 Collection ](https://www.postman.com/meraki-api/workspace/cisco-meraki-s-public-workspace/collection/897512-42355ad3-0145-48c6-8e65-e4bb9f180939) to get started.  
  
Here are a few basic API calls to get you started:

1. **List Organizations:**
    - **GET** `/organizations`
        1. _Tip: Create an environment variable for_ `organizationId` _to use in organization operations_
2. **List Networks:**
    - **GET** `/organizations/{organizationId}/networks`
    - Replace `{organizationId}` with the actual ID of your organization.
    - Fetches a list of networks under a specific organization.
        1. _Tip: Create an environment variable for_ `networkId` _to use in network operations_
3. **Get Network Devices:**
    - **GET** `/networks/{networkId}/devices`
    - Replace `{networkId}` with the ID of your network.
    - Lists the devices in a given network, providing insights into your network's infrastructure.
        1. _Tip: Create an environment variable for_ `serial` _to use in device operations_

### Resources

**Learn More About the API:**

- [Meraki Postman Collection Guide](https://developer.cisco.com/meraki/build/meraki-postman-collection-getting-started/introduction/#dashboard-api-and-postman): A detailed guide on how to utilize the Postman Collection to manage your Meraki network effectively.
- [Meraki.io](https://developer.cisco.com/meraki/api/): The official developer hub for all things Meraki API, including documentation, guides, and SDKs.
- [What's New](https://developer.cisco.com/meraki/whats-new/): Stay updated with the latest features and enhancements in the Meraki API.
- [Meraki API Community](https://meraki.io/community): Join the developer discussions

data.name: X API Public Workspace
data.summary: The X API workspace contains Collections curated by the X Developer Platform teams.
data.description:
To learn more about the X API:

- visit our [developer documentation](https://docs.x.com)
    
- check out our [sample code on GitHub](https://github.com/xdevplatform)
    
- talk to us about the API in [our developer forums](https://devcommunity.x.com)
    
- [follow us on X](https://twitter.com/TwitterDev)

data.name: WhatsApp Business Platform
data.summary: The WhatsApp Business Platform offers APIs for Postman that allow you to programmatically send and receive messages to your own systems.
data.description:
Welcome to Meta's official Postman collections for the [WhatsApp Business Platform](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp)!

Each collection below contains examples of common queries with our various APIs. These will give you a solid foundation for understanding how each of our APIs work, and will make it easier for you to begin testing our APIs so you can integrate WhatsApp business messaging services into your own applications.

## WhatsApp Cloud API

The official WhatsApp API. 

- [WhatsApp Cloud API collection](https://www.postman.com/meta/workspace/whatsapp-business-platform/collection/13382743-84d01ff8-4253-4720-b454-af661f36acc2?action=share&creator=22794852)
    
- [Cloud API developer documentation](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/cloud-api)
    

If you are a partner/business service provider looking to manage your or your clients accounts, see the Business Management API collection.

## WhatsApp Business Management API

WhatsApp Business Management API allows you to manage WhatsApp Business Accounts and their associated assets, all of which are necessary to send messages to and from WhatsApp users.

- [WhatsApp Business Management API collection](http://undefined/workspace/whatsapp-business-platform/collection/13382743-2fd9b32d-f63c-4056-873e-4c398dde9d6d?action=share&creator=22794852)
    
- [Business Management API developer documentation](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/business-management-api)
    

## WhatsApp Flows API

WhatsApp Flows API allows you to create structured, rich interactions in WhatsApp messages that make it easier for your customers to perform common business interactions, such as appointment booking, product browsing, and feedback submission.

- [WhatsApp Flows API collection](http://undefined/workspace/whatsapp-business-platform/collection/24926895-7bf51205-92ed-49d1-af4a-0130cf84b6f6?action=share&creator=22794852)
    
- [WhatsApp Flows API developer documentation](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/flows)
    

## Embedded Signup

Embedded Signup is our onboarding solution that Solution Partners, Tech Providers, and Tech Partners can use to onboard new businesses.

- [Embedded Signup collection](http://undefined/workspace/whatsapp-business-platform/collection/13382743-6da4508c-c36d-4e4c-bf74-a6fb41755c26?action=share&creator=22794852)
    
- [Embedded Signup developer documentation](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/embedded-signup)
    

## WhatsApp On-Premises API (Deprecated)

WhatsApp On-Premises API is being [deprecated](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/on-premises/sunset). Please use Cloud API instead.

WhatsApp On-Premises API is our downloadable API which you must host on your own servers.

- [WhatsApp On-Premises API collection](http://undefined/workspace/whatsapp-business-platform/collection/14291747-68ddd7f8-e8e6-4149-93dd-cecbafd3706e?action=share&creator=22794852&active-environment=14291747-787fc141-f349-47d9-90d9-6165407d757f)
    
- [On-Premises API developer documentation](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/on-premises/)
